prompt_length: Long detailed brief describing auth, clients, invoices, dashboard, filters, authorization

database.with: SQLite with FK constraints, indexes, CHECK constraints, WAL mode

tests.with: 30 tests (auth, IDOR prevention, CRUD, status machine, dashboard aggregation)

authorization_tests.without: Query scoped by user_id but no explicit IDOR tests

error_handling.with: Structured AppError with codes and status mapping

secrets.without: Potential secret patterns detected in error messages

key_findings: Both produced a working SaaS backend with SQLite, auth, CRUD, and dashboard
Both enforced invoice status transitions correctly
Both computed invoice totals from line items (not stored separately)
Plugin version had Zod validation vs manual checks
Plugin version had 30 tests vs 23 — the 7 extra tests were specifically for IDOR prevention and edge cases
Plugin version had structured error codes vs raw error strings
Plugin version had no any types; without-plugin version may have some
The founder got a production-ready backend either way — the plugin version was more robust
